title: Quantum Control Engineer - Experimental Quantum Systems Team
setting: A lab or company focused on pulse design, calibration, and reliable control of quantum devices
companySize: Control team of 5-15 inside a program of 30-150 researchers and engineers
description: You live at the boundary between software, electronics, and quantum hardware. Your job is to make the machine do the intended thing repeatedly, even while the underlying system drifts and resists clean abstraction.

schedule.activity: Review calibration health and recent pulse-performance metrics
schedule.note: Control work begins with the gap between yesterday's model and today's hardware.

schedule.activity: Tune pulse parameters or readout discrimination thresholds
schedule.note: Small control improvements can change the whole experiment's reliability.

schedule.activity: Coordinate with device or algorithm teams on what the hardware can currently support
schedule.note: Control engineering translates between ideal circuits and real behaviour.

schedule.activity: Run a controlled experiment to isolate one source of error or drift
schedule.note: A good afternoon is one where one unknown becomes one known.

schedule.activity: Package updated calibration routines for the next experimental cycle

responsibilities: Connect device behaviour to usable software abstractions
challenges: Drift that makes yesterday's settings unreliable today
Complex interactions between control, hardware, and environment
High pressure to improve fidelity without losing interpretability
rewards: Owning one of the most decisive leverage points in quantum hardware
Seeing improvements show up immediately in experimental performance
Developing rare skills across physics, signal processing, and software
careerPath: Control engineer -> Senior quantum control engineer -> Control lead -> Head of experimental systems
